popup.loading.title: Loading…
popup.loading.body: One moment — picking up the latest status.
popup.idle.label: No target yet
popup.monitoring.lookAgain: No slots. I'll look again in {time}.
popup.slotFound.evidenceLabel: Why I think there's a slot
popup.loggedOut.title: Session expired
popup.loggedOut.body: Your TLScontact session expired — {emph}. Sign back in and I'll pick up where I left off.
popup.loggedOut.bodyEmph: I can't watch a logged-out page
popup.loggedOut.note: Monitoring paused until I see a fresh session.
popup.cloudflare.title: Security check needed
popup.cloudflare.body: Cloudflare is asking you to prove you're human. That's normal — {emph}, then I'll resume polling.
popup.cloudflare.whyNot: Why I'm not solving it for you
popup.cloudflare.whyNotBody: Auto-solving CAPTCHAs is the fastest way to get an account banned. I won't do it. You always solve, I always wait.
popup.paused.title: Paused
popup.paused.label: Status
popup.paused.sub: Nothing is being checked. Last status: no slots, {time}.
popup.unknown.title: Help me read this page
popup.unknown.body: The page changed shape. {emph} and tell me what you see — I'll learn and stop asking.
popup.unknown.submit: Submit hash to help improve detection
popup.unknown.context: Visa Master watches your TLScontact appointment-booking page for slot openings. This page isn't one I recognize — likely a different step of the workflow.
popup.unknown.classifyLabel: Already on the right page? Tell me what you see and I'll learn it:
popup.idle.howItWorks: Visa Master only watches when you're on a TLScontact appointment-booking page. Open one and I'll start monitoring automatically.
welcome.hl.watch.title: Watches 24/7
welcome.hl.watch.sub: Polls TLScontact every 2–15 minutes around the clock — slot drops are caught within minutes.
welcome.hl.alert.title: Desktop + phone
welcome.hl.alert.sub: Local desktop notification by default. Optional Telegram pings to your phone when you step away from the laptop.
welcome.hl.local.title: 100% local
welcome.hl.local.sub: No servers. No telemetry. Your TLScontact session and cookies never leave your browser.
welcome.hl.free.title: Free & open source
welcome.hl.free.sub: MIT licensed. Every line of code running on your machine is in the public GitHub repo — audit it yourself.
welcome.h1.after: page so you don't have to.
welcome.lede: I'm a small, polite background process. I refresh your appointment booking page on a steady cadence and tap you on the shoulder the moment a slot appears — nothing more.
welcome.yes.1: Refresh your TLScontact booking page on a polite cadence — every 2–15 minutes, defaulting to four.
welcome.yes.2: Notify you the instant a slot opens, via desktop alert and an optional chime.
welcome.yes.3: Run entirely inside your browser. Your session never leaves this computer.
welcome.yes.4: Show my work — every detection comes with the evidence I used.
welcome.no.1: Read your passport number, name, or any form field on the page.
welcome.no.2: Book the slot for you. You always do the booking yourself.
welcome.no.3: Solve Cloudflare captchas. I'll politely ask you to.
welcome.no.4: Share your data with anyone. There is no server. There is no telemetry by default.
welcome.disclaim: TLScontact's terms of service may classify automation as prohibited. I keep polling polite to minimise risk, but you use this at your own risk. I'm open source — verify what I do at
settings.title: Settings
settings.target.none: No target detected — open a TLScontact page in any tab.
settings.detection.monthCyclingSub: Experimental — when no slots are visible in the current month, click the next month tab to scan it too. Off by default to keep the extension a passive scanner.
settings.notif.title: Change tab title and favicon
settings.notif.focus: Auto-focus the TLS tab on detection
settings.oc.encrypt: Encrypt OpenClaw token with a passphrase
settings.tg.enable: Send slot alerts to my phone via Telegram
settings.tg.enableSub: Free, encrypted, takes ~2 minutes to set up. Phone keeps notifications even when this laptop is asleep.
settings.tg.intro: 5 steps, around 2 minutes. Follow them in order — each one builds on the previous.
settings.tg.alsoBlockers: Also alert me on Cloudflare check / logged-out
settings.tg.alsoMonitoring: Ping me when monitoring starts or resumes
settings.tg.alsoMonitoringSub: A short confirmation after the extension begins watching — useful so you know it's running before you close the laptop.
settings.tg.step.install.title: Install Telegram on your phone
settings.tg.step.install.body: Telegram is a free messaging app. If you don't already have it, install it on your phone — that's where slot alerts will arrive.
settings.tg.step.install.signin: After installing, sign in with your phone number, then come back here.
settings.tg.step.bot.title: Create your bot in Telegram
settings.tg.step.bot.body: In Telegram, open the chat with @BotFather, send /newbot, and follow the prompts. BotFather will reply with a long bot token — copy it (long-press → Copy).
settings.tg.step.token.title: Paste the bot token here
settings.tg.step.token.body: Paste the raw token. No quotes, no angle brackets, no spaces.
settings.tg.step.token.tipTitle: Common mistake — don't include angle brackets
settings.tg.step.token.urlGoodLabel: Correct — your real token, no brackets:
settings.tg.step.token.urlBadLabel: Wrong — angle brackets are placeholders, not part of the URL:
settings.tg.step.token.okHint: After you message your bot (Step 4), click below — it opens your Chat ID page with your real token already filled in.
settings.tg.step.start.title: Open your new bot and say hi
settings.tg.step.start.body: BotFather's reply contains a link to your bot (looks like t.me/your_bot_name). Open it in Telegram, tap Start, or send any message. This is what creates the chat — without it, the next step returns nothing.
settings.tg.step.chatid.title: Get your Chat ID and send a test
settings.tg.step.chatid.body: Click below — a JSON page opens in a new tab. Find chat.id and copy the number.
settings.tg.step.chatid.chatIdHint: Numeric — paste the chat.id from the JSON page above.
settings.tg.step.chatid.findChatIdDisabl: Paste your bot token in Step 3 first.
settings.tg.step.chatid.responseLabel: What you'll see — copy the chat.id number:
settings.tg.preview.title: What you'll receive on your phone
settings.tg.preview.intro: Once you're set up, Visa Master pushes these alerts to your Telegram bot.
settings.tg.preview.slotBody:
🚨 Slot found — Manchester
FR visa · gbMNC2fr
15:21:08 UTC

Switch to your TLScontact tab and book now.
settings.tg.preview.cfLabel: Cloudflare check needed — only if you turn on the toggle above
settings.tg.preview.cfBody:
⚠️ Cloudflare check needed
Manchester · 15:21:08 UTC

Open the TLS tab and complete the check. Monitoring is paused.
settings.tg.preview.loLabel: Session expired — only if you turn on the toggle above
settings.tg.preview.loBody:
⚠️ TLScontact session expired
Manchester · 15:21:08 UTC

Sign back in to resume monitoring.
settings.tg.preview.monLabel: Monitoring started — only if you turn on the toggle above
settings.tg.preview.monBody:
✅ Monitoring started — Manchester
FR visa · gbMNC2fr
15:21:08 UTC

I'll ping you the moment a slot opens.
settings.tg.preview.privacyIncludes: Each message contains: centre name, country code (e.g. FR), subject code (e.g. gbMNC2fr), and a timestamp.
settings.tg.preview.privacyExcludes: Never sent to Telegram: your TLScontact login, cookies, passport number, URLs, or any form data on the page.
settings.lang.note: Detection language must match TLScontact account setting.
settings.privacy.telemetrySub: Sent: poll counts, error counts. Never: URLs, PII.
settings.about.developerSub: Torly AI builds visa-application AI assistants — including a UK Innovator Founder visa AI-assistant and this Schengen Visa Master agent. Open source so you can audit what runs on your machine.
wizard.title: Pair with OpenClaw gateway
wizard.step1.heading: Step 1 of 2 — Gateway address
wizard.step1.label: Where is your OpenClaw gateway running?
wizard.step1.hint: Most local setups use the default. For remote, use wss://your-gateway.example.com/openclaw
wizard.step1.invalid: Must be a ws:// or wss:// URL.
wizard.step2.heading: Step 2 of 2 — Pairing token
wizard.step2.run: Run this command on your OpenClaw host:
lang.en.label: English
lang.zh.label: 中文
